question.text: Under Rule 9 of the PML Rules, client due diligence must be conducted at the time of commencement of an account-based relationship or when carrying out occasional transactions of an amount equal to or exceeding:

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Rule 9(1) requires CDD at the time of commencement of an account-based relationship or while carrying out occasional transactions of an amount equal to or exceeding Rs. 50,000 (whether as a single transaction or several connected transactions), or any international money transfer operations. The three CDD requirements are: identify and verify client identity, understand the client's business/ownership/control, and determine beneficial ownership.
**The Trap:** Rs. 50,000 is the threshold for occasional transactions triggering CDD, not Rs. 10,000 or Rs. 1 lakh. For account-based relationships, CDD is mandatory regardless of the transaction amount. For occasional transactions, CDD kicks in at Rs. 50,000.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV tests the same Rs. 50,000 threshold. The IFSCA exam may test this alongside the IFSCA-specific CDD requirements for regulated entities.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Two triggers for CDD: (1) any new account-based relationship (no threshold), (2) occasional transactions of Rs. 50,000 or more. For IFSC entities, most relationships are account-based, so CDD is virtually always required from the start.
